2314	\section{Support and freeness predicates}
2315	\label{sec:quot_predicates}
2316	
2317	The Quot functor (\cref{def:quot_functor}) and the Grassmannian
2318	(\cref{def:grassmannian_scheme}) classify quotient sheaves subject to two
2319	geometric conditions: a coherent quotient must have \emph{schematic support
2320	proper over the base}, and the Grassmannian's quotients must be \emph{locally
2321	free of a fixed rank}. This section introduces both predicates.
2322	
2323	\begin{definition}
2324	
2325	\leanok
2326	[Annihilator ideal sheaf of a sheaf of modules]
2327	  \label{def:modules_annihilator}
2328	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator}
2329	  % NOTE: The definition carries no \uses: the Lean construction is
2330	  % `IdealSheafData.ofIdeals (fun U => Module.annihilator Γ(X,U) Γ(F,U))`, which is well-defined
2331	  % unconditionally and does NOT depend on the localization bridges at definition time. The two
2332	  % facts lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map and lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen are needed
2333	  % only for the downstream CHARACTERIZATION (the reverse inclusion / equality on affine opens, the
2334	  % "annihilator forward direction"), which is not yet a blueprint block.
2335	  % NOTE: The definition itself is formalized and axiom-clean; the inclusion direction
2336	  % \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U) \subseteq \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(\mathcal{F}(U))\)
2337	  % (\cref{lem:modules_annihilator_ideal_le}) is closed. The reverse inclusion, which identifies
2338	  % the annihilator sheaf with the module-annihilator on affine opens, remains bridge-gated on
2339	  % \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}.
2340	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
2341	  % Polynomials" (read from
2342	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L468-L471).
2343	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2344	  % "Let $X\to S$ be a finite type morphism of noetherian schemes,
2345	  %  and let $L$ be a line bundle on $X$. Let $\F$
2346	  %  be any coherent sheaf on
2347	  %  $X$ whose schematic support is proper over $S$."
2348	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
2349	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a sheaf of
2350	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules that is \emph{quasi-coherent and of finite type}
2351	  (over a locally noetherian base, so that on each affine open \(U\) the section
2352	  module \(\mathcal{F}(U)\) is a finitely generated \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-module).
2353	  The \emph{annihilator ideal sheaf} of \(\mathcal{F}\) is the ideal sheaf
2354	  \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\) defined on affine opens: for each affine open
2355	  \(U \subseteq X\),
2356	  \[
2357	    \mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U)
2358	    \;=\; \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}\bigl(\mathcal{F}(U)\bigr)
2359	    \;\subseteq\; \mathcal{O}_X(U),
2360	  \]
2361	  the annihilator of the \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-module \(\mathcal{F}(U)\). These
2362	  local data patch to a coherent ideal sheaf on \(X\) whose vanishing locus is
2363	  the set-theoretic support of \(\mathcal{F}\).
2364	
2365	  \emph{Well-definedness on basic opens.} The coherence condition for an ideal
2366	  sheaf requires that, for an affine open \(U\) and \(f \in \mathcal{O}_X(U)\),
2367	  the section \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U)\) restricts correctly to the basic
2368	  open \(D(f) \subseteq U\), i.e. that
2369	  \(\mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(D(f))}(\mathcal{F}(D(f)))\) is the extension of
2370	  \(\mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(\mathcal{F}(U))\) along the localization map
2371	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(U) \to \mathcal{O}_X(D(f))\). This is exactly where the two
2372	  hypotheses enter. The bridge
2373	  \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen} supplies, for a quasi-coherent
2374	  finite-type \(\mathcal{F}\), that \(\mathcal{O}_X(D(f)) = (\mathcal{O}_X(U))_f\)
2375	  and that the restriction \(\mathcal{F}(U) \to \mathcal{F}(D(f))\) exhibits
2376	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(f))\) as the localized module \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}
2377	  \mathcal{F}(U)\); the algebra engine
2378	  \cref{lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map} then yields, using that
2379	  \(\mathcal{F}(U)\) is finitely generated, the required identity
2380	  \(\mathrm{Ann}_{(\mathcal{O}_X(U))_f}\bigl((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}
2381	  \mathcal{F}(U)\bigr) = \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(\mathcal{F}(U)) \cdot
2382	  (\mathcal{O}_X(U))_f\). Thus the local annihilators are compatible with basic-open
2383	  restriction, which is the patching datum that makes \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\)
2384	  a well-defined ideal sheaf. The finite-type hypothesis is genuinely required:
2385	  without finite generation of \(\mathcal{F}(U)\) the annihilator need not commute
2386	  with localization, and the local data fail to patch.
2387	
2388	  \emph{Note.} Mathlib does not carry an annihilator for sheaves of modules on a scheme.
2389	\end{definition}

2391	\begin{lemma}\leanok
2392	[Annihilator ideal bounded by the module annihilator on affine opens]
2393	  \label{lem:modules_annihilator_ideal_le}
2394	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator_ideal_le}
2395	  \uses{def:modules_annihilator}
2396	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\mathcal{F}\) a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and
2397	  \(U \subseteq X\) an affine open. Then the section over \(U\) of the annihilator
2398	  ideal sheaf \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\) (\cref{def:modules_annihilator}) is
2399	  contained in the module-annihilator of the sections of \(\mathcal{F}\) over
2400	  \(U\):
2401	  \[
2402	    \mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U)
2403	    \;\subseteq\; \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}\bigl(\mathcal{F}(U)\bigr).
2404	  \]
2405	  This is the inclusion direction available directly from the definition of
2406	  \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\); the reverse inclusion is the basic-open coherence
2407	  statement gated on \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}. Proved directly
2408	  in Lean. It supports the well-definedness of the schematic support
2409	  (\cref{def:schematic_support}).
2410	\end{lemma}
2411	
2412	\begin{lemma}\leanok
2413	[Annihilator of a finitely generated module commutes with
2414	  localization]
2415	  \label{lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map}
2416	  \lean{Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map}
2417	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, let \(S \subseteq R\) be a multiplicative
2418	  submonoid, and let \(R_S = S^{-1}R\) be the localization of \(R\) at \(S\). Let
2419	  \(M\) be a \emph{finitely generated} \(R\)-module and let \(f : M \to M_S\) be
2420	  an \(R\)-linear map exhibiting \(M_S\) as the localized module \(S^{-1}M\)
2421	  (i.e.\ \(f\) is a localization of \(M\) at \(S\)). Then the annihilator of
2422	  \(M_S\) over \(R_S\) is the extension of the annihilator of \(M\) along the
2423	  structure map \(R \to R_S\):
2424	  \[
2425	    \mathrm{Ann}_{R_S}(M_S)
2426	    \;=\; (\mathrm{Ann}_R M)\cdot R_S
2427	    \;=\; \mathrm{map}\bigl(R \to R_S\bigr)\bigl(\mathrm{Ann}_R M\bigr).
2428	  \]
2429	  Equivalently, for finitely generated \(M\) the annihilator commutes with
2430	  localization: \(\mathrm{Ann}(S^{-1}M) = S^{-1}\,\mathrm{Ann}(M)\).
2431	
2432	  \emph{The finite-generation hypothesis is essential.} For a module that is not
2433	  finitely generated the inclusion \(S^{-1}\,\mathrm{Ann}(M) \subseteq
2434	  \mathrm{Ann}(S^{-1}M)\) can be strict, so the identity fails. This is the
2435	  standard commutative-algebra fact that the annihilator of a finite module
2436	  localizes; it is recorded here as the load-bearing algebra engine for the
2437	  basic-open coherence of \cref{def:modules_annihilator}.
2438	\end{lemma}
2439	
2440	\begin{proof}
2441	  \(\supseteq\): if \(r \in \mathrm{Ann}_R M\), then \(r\) kills every element of
2442	  \(M\), hence its image \(r/1 \in R_S\) kills every element of \(M_S\) (each such
2443	  element is \(m/s = (1/s)\cdot f(m)\) for some \(m \in M\), \(s \in S\), and
2444	  \((r/1)\cdot(m/s) = (1/s)\cdot f(r\,m) = 0\)). Thus
2445	  \((\mathrm{Ann}_R M)\cdot R_S \subseteq \mathrm{Ann}_{R_S}(M_S)\).
2446	
2447	  \(\subseteq\): write \(M = \langle m_1, \dots, m_n\rangle\) using the
2448	  finite-generation hypothesis, and let \(y \in \mathrm{Ann}_{R_S}(M_S)\). Write
2449	  \(y = a/s\) with \(a \in R\), \(s \in S\). For each generator \(m_i\) the element
2450	  \(y\cdot f(m_i) = (a/s)\cdot f(m_i) = 0\) in \(M_S\); by the definition of the
2451	  localized module this means there is \(u_i \in S\) with \(u_i\,a\,m_i = 0\) in
2452	  \(M\). Put \(u = \prod_{i=1}^n u_i \in S\). Then \(u\,a\) kills every generator
2453	  \(m_i\), hence kills all of \(M\), so \(u\,a \in \mathrm{Ann}_R M\). Finally
2454	  \(y = a/s = (u\,a)/(u\,s)\) lies in \(S^{-1}\,\mathrm{Ann}_R M =
2455	  (\mathrm{Ann}_R M)\cdot R_S\). Therefore \(\mathrm{Ann}_{R_S}(M_S) \subseteq
2456	  (\mathrm{Ann}_R M)\cdot R_S\), and combined with the reverse inclusion the two
2457	  ideals coincide. The use of a single common multiplier \(u = \prod_i u_i\) is
2458	  exactly the step that requires finitely many generators.
2459	\end{proof}

2461	\begin{lemma}[Sections on a basic open localize the affine ring]
2462	  \label{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
2463	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_basicOpen}
2464	  \mathlibok
2465	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
2466	  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme}).}

2468	  \(f \in \Gamma(X, U)\). Then the restriction map
2469	  \(\Gamma(X, U) \to \Gamma\bigl(X,\, D(f)\bigr)\) on the basic open
2470	  \(D(f) = X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,f\) exhibits \(\Gamma(X, D(f))\) as the
2471	  localization of \(\Gamma(X, U)\) away from \(f\); that is, it is

2473	  \(\Gamma(X, D(f)) = (\Gamma(X, U))_f\) as a \(\Gamma(X,U)\)-algebra.
2474	\end{lemma}

2523	\begin{proof}
2524	  \uses{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib, lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}
2525	  Part (1) is \cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}, the Mathlib statement
2526	  that on an affine open \(U\) the sections over the basic open \(D(f)\) form the
2527	  localization of \(\Gamma(X,U)\) away from \(f\).
2528	
2529	  Part (2) is the \emph{gap-2 transport}: it reduces to G1-core
2530	  (\cref{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}) on the single affine chart
2531	  \(\Spec \Gamma(X, U)\). Since \(U\) is affine, Mathlib's canonical isomorphism
2532	  \(\varphi : U \xrightarrow{\sim} \Spec \Gamma(X, U)\) identifies \(U\) with the
2533	  spectrum of its ring of sections. Push \(\mathcal{M}|_U\) forward along
2534	  \(\varphi\) to a sheaf of modules \(\mathcal{M}'\) on \(\Spec \Gamma(X, U)\);
2535	  because \(\mathcal{M}\) is quasi-coherent and quasi-coherence is preserved by
2536	  pullback/pushforward along an isomorphism, \(\mathcal{M}'\) is quasi-coherent.
2537	  Under \(\varphi\) the basic open \(D(f) \subseteq U\) corresponds to the standard
2538	  basic open \(D(f) \subseteq \Spec \Gamma(X, U)\) (the structure-sheaf section
2539	  \(f \in \Gamma(X, U)\) transports to \(f \in \Gamma(\Spec\Gamma(X,U), \top)\)),
2540	  and the section restriction \(\mathcal{M}(U) \to \mathcal{M}(D(f))\) corresponds,
2541	  under the iso on sections, to the restriction
2542	  \(\Gamma(\mathcal{M}', \top) \to \Gamma(\mathcal{M}', D(f))\).
2543	
2544	  Now apply G1-core (\cref{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}) to the
2545	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{M}'\) on the affine \(\Spec \Gamma(X, U)\) and the
2546	  element \(f\): the restriction
2547	  \(\Gamma(\mathcal{M}', \top) \to \Gamma(\mathcal{M}', D(f))\) is
2548	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over \(\Gamma(X, U)\).
2549	  Transporting this localization property back across the iso on sections induced
2550	  by \(\varphi\) (using the standard transfer of \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}\)
2551	  along the pre/post-composing linear equivalences) shows the restriction
2552	  \(\mathcal{M}(U) \to \mathcal{M}(D(f))\) is itself a localization at
2553	  \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\).
2554	
2555	  \emph{Remaining ingredient.} The chapter does not yet package the transport of
2556	  the \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}\) predicate across the affine identification
2557	  \(\varphi : U \cong \Spec \Gamma(X, U)\) (carrying \(\mathcal{M}|_U\) to the
2558	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{M}'\) and \(D(f)\) to the standard \(D(f)\)) as a named
2559	  lemma; there is no Mathlib lemma performing this specific transport. This is the
2560	  sole genuinely new piece the prover must supply on top of G1-core --- a
2561	  \emph{single-chart} affine-isomorphism transport, \emph{not} a cover-and-glue
2562	  over a family of affine opens (\(U\) is already affine, so no second cover of
2563	  \(X\) is introduced and no ``infinite onion'' of nested gluings arises). It is
2564	  local on \(U\) in the trivial sense that there is one chart.
2565	\end{proof}

2568	[Basic-open restriction of a $\widetilde{N}$ sheaf is a module localization]
2569	  \label{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}

3628	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", \S ``Sections over principal opens'',
3629	  Lemma \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections}; cf.\ [Hartshorne], II.5.3.}
3630	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) and \(f \in R\). Suppose given a finite basic-open
3631	  cover of \(\Spec R\): a finite set \(t \subseteq R\) with \(\mathrm{span}(t) = (1)\), so that
3632	  \(\{D(r)\}_{r \in t}\) covers \(\Spec R\). Suppose moreover the \emph{per-piece localization data}
3633	  \[
3634	    \mathrm{Hfr} : \quad \text{for every open } U \le D(r) \text{ for some } r \in t, \quad
3635	    \bigl(\Gamma(M, U) \longrightarrow \Gamma(M,\, D(f) \sqcap U)\bigr)
3636	    \text{ is } \mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f),
3637	  \]
3638	  i.e.\ on every open contained in a cover member -- in particular each \(D(r)\) and each overlap
3639	  \(D(r) \sqcap D(r')\) -- the restriction to the \(f\)-locus exhibits the target as the localization
3640	  of the source at the powers of \(f\). Then the global section restriction
3641	  \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) is itself \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\)
3642	  over \(R\); that is, \(\Gamma(M, D(f)) \cong (\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\,\Gamma(M, \top)\).
3643	\end{lemma}
3644	% NOTE: the lemma STATEMENT is cited to Stacks lemma-invert-f-sections / Hartshorne II.5.3; the proof
3645	% below is the project-internal finite-cover descent (gluing + separatedness + flatness of
3646	% localization) that deliberately does NOT route through the global affine equivalence (which is gap1
3647	% itself), so there is no transcribed `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF`.
3648	\begin{proof}
3649	  \uses{lem:map_units_restrict_basicOpen, lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib,
3650	    lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib, lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib,
3651	    lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib}
3652	  We verify the three fields of \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) for the restriction
3653	  \(\theta : \Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\).
3654	
3655	  \emph{\(\mathrm{map\_units}\).} For every \(n\) the element \(f^n\) acts invertibly on
3656	  \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\): on the basic open \(D(f)\) the global section \(f\) is a unit of
3657	  \(\Gamma(\mathcal{O}, D(f)) = R_f\), and scalar multiplication by a unit is bijective. This holds
3658	  for an arbitrary sheaf of modules \(M\) and is \cref{lem:map_units_restrict_basicOpen}; it does not
3659	  use \(\mathrm{Hfr}\).
3660	
3661	  \emph{\(\mathrm{surj}\) (every section over \(D(f)\) is a fraction).} Let
3662	  \(y \in \Gamma(M, D(f))\). On each cover member \(D(r)\), the restriction
3663	  \(y|_{D(f) \sqcap D(r)}\) lies in the localization \(\Gamma(M, D(f) \sqcap D(r))\) of
3664	  \(\Gamma(M, D(r))\) at \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\) (the case \(U = D(r)\) of \(\mathrm{Hfr}\)), so there
3665	  are \(x_r \in \Gamma(M, D(r))\) and \(n_r\) with \(f^{n_r}\, y|_{D(f)\sqcap D(r)} = x_r|_{D(f)\sqcap
3666	  D(r)}\). Taking a common exponent \(N \ge \max_r n_r\) (the cover is finite) and rescaling, we may
3667	  assume \(f^{N} y\) is represented on each \(D(r)\) by a section \(\tilde x_r\). On an overlap
3668	  \(D(r) \sqcap D(r')\) the two representatives \(\tilde x_r, \tilde x_{r'}\) restrict to sections of
3669	  \(\Gamma(M, D(r) \sqcap D(r'))\) that agree after further multiplication by \(f\): both compute
3670	  \(f^{N} y\) on \(D(f) \sqcap D(r) \sqcap D(r')\), and the overlap restriction
3671	  \(\Gamma(M, D(r)\sqcap D(r')) \to \Gamma(M, D(f) \sqcap D(r) \sqcap D(r'))\) is
3672	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) (the case \(U = D(r) \sqcap D(r')\) of
3673	  \(\mathrm{Hfr}\)), hence injective up to a power of \(f\); so there is a common further exponent
3674	  \(P\) with \(f^{P}\tilde x_r\) and \(f^{P}\tilde x_{r'}\) equal on the overlap. The family
3675	  \(\{f^{P}\tilde x_r\}_r\) is then compatible on overlaps and glues, by the sheaf condition over the
3676	  finite cover (\cref{lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}), to a global section
3677	  \(x \in \Gamma(M, \top)\). Its restriction to \(D(f)\) equals \(f^{N+P} y\): the two agree on each
3678	  \(D(f) \sqcap D(r)\), and \(\{D(f)\sqcap D(r)\}_r\) covers \(D(f)\), so they agree by separatedness
3679	  (\cref{lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}). Thus \(\theta(x) = f^{N+P} y\), exhibiting \(y\) as the
3680	  fraction \(x / f^{N+P}\).
3681	
3682	  \emph{\(\mathrm{exists\_of\_eq}\) (a vanishing fraction is annihilated by a power of \(f\)).}
3683	  Let \(x \in \Gamma(M, \top)\) with \(\theta(x) = x|_{D(f)} = 0\). On each cover member \(D(r)\), the
3684	  restriction \(x|_{D(r)}\) maps to \(0\) in \(\Gamma(M, D(f) \sqcap D(r))\), so by the localization
3685	  hypothesis (the case \(U = D(r)\) of \(\mathrm{Hfr}\), which gives the \(\mathrm{exists\_of\_eq}\)
3686	  field on that piece) there is \(n_r\) with \(f^{n_r}\, x|_{D(r)} = 0\). A common exponent
3687	  \(N \ge \max_r n_r\) over the finite cover gives \(f^{N} x|_{D(r)} = 0\) for every \(r\); since the
3688	  \(D(r)\) cover \(\Spec R\), separatedness (\cref{lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}) forces
3689	  \(f^{N} x = 0\) in \(\Gamma(M, \top)\).
3690	
3691	  These three fields are exactly \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) for \(\theta\).
3692	  Equivalently the comparison \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) is an
3693	  isomorphism -- the \(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})_f \cong \Gamma(X_f, \mathcal{F})\) of Stacks
3694	  \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections} on \(X = \Spec R\), \(X_f = D(f)\), obtained from local data over a
3695	  finite cover by sheaf gluing and separatedness, with the finite limit interacting with localization
3696	  through the flatness of localization (\cref{lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib},
3697	  \cref{lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib}), never through the global affine equivalence.
3698	\end{proof}

3705	[Restriction maps of a sheaf compose (descent bookkeeping)]
3706	  \label{lem:res_comp}
3707	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.res_comp}
3708	  \textit{Project-local bookkeeping for the section-localization descent.}
3709	  For a sheaf \(F\) on \(\Spec R\) and opens \(C \le B \le A\), the composite of the restriction maps
3710	  \(\Gamma(F, A) \to \Gamma(F, B) \to \Gamma(F, C)\) equals the direct restriction
3711	  \(\Gamma(F, A) \to \Gamma(F, C)\).
3712	\end{lemma}
3713	\begin{proof}
3714	  The two poset inclusions \(C \le B \le A\) compose to \(C \le A\) in the poset of opens (uniqueness
3715	  of poset-hom), and \(F.\mathrm{map}\) is functorial, so the two presheaf maps compose to the direct
3716	  one.
3717	\end{proof}

3870	  \textit{Project-bespoke infrastructure: the section-level transport feeding the per-cover-element
3871	  hypothesis \(\mathrm{Hfr}\) of \cref{lem:section_localization_descent_of_cover}.}
3872	  Let \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion of schemes and let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules
3873	  on \(X\). The global sections of the pullback module along \(j\) are canonically isomorphic to the
3874	  sections of \(M\) over the image of \(j\):
3875	  \[
3876	    \Gamma\bigl((\mathrm{pullback}\,j).\mathrm{obj}\,M,\ \top\bigr)
3877	    \;\cong\; \Gamma\bigl(M,\ \mathrm{range}\,j\bigr),
3878	  \]
3879	  naturally in the open argument: for opens \(V \le \top\) of \(U\) the comparison
3880	  \(\Gamma((\mathrm{pullback}\,j).\mathrm{obj}\,M, V) \cong \Gamma(M, j(V))\) intertwines the
3881	  restriction maps of the two presheaves, so any two such instances commute with the restriction
3882	  between them.

3885	\begin{proof}
3886	  \uses{lem:modules_pullback_mathlib, lem:modules_restrictFunctor_mathlib,
3887	    lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
3888	  For an open immersion \(j\), Mathlib's restriction functor \(j^{\mathrm{res}}\) satisfies
3889	  \(\Gamma(j^{\mathrm{res}} M, V) = \Gamma(M, j(V))\) \emph{definitionally}
3890	  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctor_mathlib}); in particular at \(V = \top\) the right-hand side is
3891	  \(\Gamma(M, j(\top)) = \Gamma(M, \mathrm{range}\,j)\). The restriction functor is naturally
3892	  isomorphic to the universal-property pullback,
3893	  \(j^{\mathrm{res}} \cong j^{*} = \mathrm{pullback}\,j\)

3968	\begin{lemma}\leanok
3969	[Ring-iso-semilinear localized-module transport (gap1 ingredient I)]
3970	  \label{lem:isLocalizedModule_ringEquiv_semilinear}
3971	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear}
3972	  Let \(\sigma : R \xrightarrow{\sim} R'\) be a ring isomorphism, \(S \le R\) a submonoid, and
3973	  \(g : M_1 \to M_2\) an \(R\)-linear map satisfying \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}\ S\ g\). Suppose
3974	  \(e_1 : M_1 \simeq_+ N_1\) and \(e_2 : M_2 \simeq_+ N_2\) are additive isomorphisms that are
3975	  \(\sigma\)-semilinear (\(e_i(a \cdot x) = \sigma(a) \cdot e_i(x)\)), and \(h : N_1 \to N_2\) is an
3976	  \(R'\)-linear map intertwining them, \(h(e_1 x) = e_2(g x)\). Then \(h\) satisfies
3977	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}\ (S.\mathrm{map}\ \sigma)\ h\) over \(R'\).
3978	\end{lemma}
3979	\begin{proof}
3980	  Transport the three \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}\) fields of \(g\) along the semilinear pair
3981	  \((e_1,e_2)\). \emph{map\_units:} the action of \(s' = \sigma(s)\) on \(N_2\) is conjugate via \(e_2\) to
3982	  the (bijective, since a unit) action of \(s\) on \(M_2\), hence bijective, hence a unit
3983	  (\(\mathrm{Module.End.isUnit\_iff}\)). \emph{surj:} given \(y \in N_2\), pull back \(e_2^{-1}y\), apply
3984	  the surjectivity field of \(g\) to get \(g\,m = s\cdot e_2^{-1}y\), and push forward by \(e_2\); the
3985	  image-submonoid witness is \(\langle \sigma s, s, s\in S, \mathrm{rfl}\rangle\). \emph{exists\_of\_eq:}
3986	  symmetric, pulling the equality back through \(e_1\). Mathlib provides only the same-ring
3987	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule.of\_linearEquiv}\); crossing a ring iso is the genuine gap.
3988	\end{proof}

3994	  Let \(R_r\) be an \(R\)-algebra, \(f \in R\), and \(g : M_1 \to M_2\) an \(R_r\)-linear map with
3995	  \(\mathrm{IsScalarTower}\ R\ R_r\ M_i\). If \(g\) satisfies
3996	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}\ (\mathrm{powers}(\mathrm{algebraMap}\ R\ R_r\ f))\ g\) over \(R_r\), then

3998	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}\ (\mathrm{powers}\ f)\) over \(R\).

4000	\begin{proof}
4001	  Each \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}\) field transfers along the identification
4002	  \(f^n \leftrightarrow (\mathrm{algebraMap}\ R\ R_r\ f)^n\) (\(\mathrm{map\_pow}\)) and the scalar
4003	  compatibility \((\mathrm{algebraMap}\ R\ R_r\ a)\cdot m = a \cdot m\) (\(\mathrm{algebraMap\_smul}\) via
4004	  the scalar tower); the two \(\mathrm{End}\) maps are the same underlying function, so the unit/surjectivity
4005	  data transfer verbatim. This lets the \(R_r\)-level localization produced on the slice
4006	  \(\Spec R_r\) be read back as the \(R\)-level data the cover-form descent consumes.
